items.question: For y = 5 and ŷ = 3, what is the MSE?
For the same y = 5, ŷ = 3, what is the MAE?
Binary label y = 1, sigmoid ŷ = 0.25. What is the BCE (natural log)?
True class index 2, softmax ŷ = [0.1, 0.2, 0.7]. What is CE?
items.explanation: One-hot CE uses only the probability on the true class: −log ŷ_y = −log(0.7).

items.question: Which loss should you use for single-label 5-class classification?
items.explanation: Mutually exclusive multi-class labels use CE. BCE is for binary or independent multi-label; MSE is for regression.

items.question: Minimizing MSE on Gaussian noise targets is equivalent to:
options: Maximum likelihood of the conditional mean
items.answer: Maximum likelihood of the conditional mean
items.explanation: Under homoscedastic Gaussian errors, MSE minimization estimates the conditional mean μ(x) via MLE.
